message:
feat(vfs,core): wire io_uring as default Linux pager backend; fix fallback metrics

connection.rs: add PagerBackend::IoUring variant gated on target_os="linux"
so file-backed Connection::open now selects IoUringVfs on Linux and UnixVfs
on other unix platforms. All PagerBackend match arms exhaustively updated
(begin, journal_mode, set_journal_mode, install_wal_backend, checkpoint,
cache_metrics_snapshot, reset_cache_metrics, kind_str, is_file_backed,
Debug). Tests split by target_os: existing Unix assertions narrowed to
non-Linux; new IoUring assertions for Linux; hardcoded "unix" expectations
made platform-conditional via cfg!().

uring.rs: IoUringFile::read and write now record unix_fallback metric in
the else branch when runtime is None (disabled/absent), fixing silent
undercounting of unix_fallbacks_total when runtime is pre-disabled before
any I/O. New test test_disabled_runtime_records_unix_fallback_metrics
verifies both read and write paths record fallbacks after runtime.disable().

.beads/issues.jsonl: close bd-3bw.5 (RaptorQ repair/PoR acceptance
validated via rch) and bd-3u7.2 (io_uring backend with passing quality
gates); advance bd-al1 to in_progress with fallback-telemetry evidence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
